title: Even the Supreme Court Needs Public Relations
sub_topics: Anvil Awards keynote (February 23, 2001, Hotel Intercontinental Makati)
PRSP (Public Relations Society of the Philippines)
Intel Philippines Science Fair as Grand Anvil winner
Jose A. Carpio Sr. (Leni's father, PRSP founding father, Dean of Filipino PR Practitioners)
Carpio's near-success in recruiting CJ as EVP of his PR firm
Court as 'Mount Olympus where the gods dwelt'
Code of Judicial Ethics limits on public appearances
SC Committee on Public Information / Ismael Khan Jr. as PIO
Leadership by Example: The Davide Standard (1998 book)
Four Ins reiterated (integrity, intelligence, independence, industry)
SWS and Pulse Asia poll surveys naming Davide most trusted

stances.claim: Until recently, the Supreme Court was 'probably the most inscrutable government institution in this country… a fortress, a Mount Olympus where the gods dwelt far above and removed from the teeming crowd' — yet it could not 'forever resist the intrusion of the Information Age or the curiosity… of an awakened, technology-driven and people-powered citizenry.'
stances.rhetorical_move: Mount-Olympus framing of judicial inscrutability + Information-Age inevitability

stances.claim: While SC decisions are nonpolitical and not subject to popular vote, the Court 'can be effective only insofar as it retains the long-term support of the public' — public acceptance of decisions going against public opinion is possible only when the Tribunal enjoys 'people's residual respect and esteem.'
stances.rhetorical_move: structural-dependency framing (countermajoritarian institution needs majoritarian legitimacy)

stances.claim: The Court's PIO objective is 'simply to tell others the truth about the Supreme Court and its work' — not to 'sanitize or angle stories,' not to 'proselytize and propaganize,' not to 'spin tales or embroider'; the goal is 'the truth, the whole truth, and nothing but the truth.'
stances.rhetorical_move: truth-as-PR-objective framing + structured negation of common PR vices

stances.claim: An abstract institution needs a human face — Davide is the 'representation or human symbol' of the SC; CJ wrote 'Leadership by Example: The Davide Standard' shortly after Davide's 1998 assumption to make that symbol known, with 3,000 copies distributed to decision-makers, government officials, media, libraries, business leaders, and NGOs.
stances.rhetorical_move: human-face framing of institutional communication + structured-distribution evidence

stances.claim: Davide is the model judge personifying the four Ins (integrity, intelligence, independence, industry) — SWS and Pulse Asia surveys name him the most trusted Filipino today, and the Inquirer named him 'Filipino of the Year 2000.'

A litigation is won, not because one side gets more cheers from the gallery, but because it is upheld by the law through a process of reasoning — the SC's public-relations need is not for popularity but for residual respect.

institutions: Public Relations Society of the Philippines
events: Jose A. Carpio Sr.'s posthumous Gawad Panday award (August 31, 1998)

notable_anecdotes: Leni's father Jose A. Carpio Sr. — PRSP founding father, first Filipino elected to active membership in PR Society of America, 'Dean of Filipino PR Practitioners,' posthumous Gawad Panday recipient (1998 with Pres. Ramos)

Father-in-law Jose Carpio Sr. 'nearly succeeded in convincing me during my fledgling years, while still an underpaid and overworked law practitioner, to join his public relations firm as executive vice president' — CJ refused due to 'Kapampangan stubborness'
Writing 'Leadership by Example: The Davide Standard' shortly after Davide's 1998 assumption + distributing 3,000 copies to decision-makers etc
Pre-impeachment-trial radio-TV interview standard line: 'I assure you that Chief Justice Davide will preside over the impeachment trial in a manner all of us Filipinos will be proud of'
